methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $17.6B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Endurosat is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $167M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers at the same stage (Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, Sift) and 5 public space & aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL). Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue data.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The peer-median approach across 4 Series B space companies yields $700M, with a range of $274M to $1.3B. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, anchoring from a $17.6B raised-proxy baseline, implying $33.4B. This wide divergence reflects methodological sensitivity: the peer set represents observable market pricing for comparable-stage assets, while the stage-progression method is highly dependent on the baseline proxy assumption and multiplier selection.

The triangulated valuation range is $595M (low), $17.1B (median), and $38.5B (high). The peer-median of $700M represents the most conservative, market-grounded estimate. Critical uncertainties include: the appropriateness of the $17.6B raised-proxy baseline, the relevance of the four peer comps given Endurosat's specific operational focus, and whether stage-progression multipliers derived from broader venture datasets apply to frontier aerospace. A valuation in the $600M–$1.3B range aligns more closely with directly comparable private transactions.
